system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

ddtSchemes

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,omega)  Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;

}

laplacianSchemes

}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{

}

wallDist

}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='momentumTransport', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      momentumTransport;
}

simulationType  RAS;

RAS
{
    model           realizableKEpsilon;
    turbulence      on;
    printCoeffs     on;
}'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        agglomerator    faceAreaPair;
        nPreSweeps      0;
        nPostSweeps     2;
        cacheAgglomeration yes;
        nCellsInCoarsestLevel 10;
        mergeLevels     1;
        maxIter         10;
        consistent      yes;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
        maxIter         10;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
        bounded         yes;
        bounds          (0 0.06);
        maxIter         10;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          1e-06;
        maxIter         50;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         1;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;

    residualControl
    {
        U           1e-06;
        p           1e-06;
        k           1e-06;
        epsilon     1e-06;
    }

    relaxationFactors
    {
        fields
        {
            U           0.3;
            p           0.2;
            k           0.3;
            epsilon     0.3;
        }
        equations
        {
            U           0.3;
            p           0.2;
            k           0.3;
            epsilon     0.3;
        }
    }
}

adjustTimeStep      yes;

maxCo               0.5;

maxDeltaT           0.0001;

<review_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error is clear and straightforward:

```
Unknown RASModel type realizableKEpsilon

Valid RASModel types:
19
(
LRR
LamBremhorstKE
LaunderSharmaKE
LienCubicKE
LienLeschziner
RNGkEpsilon
SSG
ShihQuadraticKE
SpalartAllmaras
kEpsilon
kOmega
kOmega2006
kOmegaSST
kOmegaSSTLM
kOmegaSSTSAS
kkLOmega
qZeta
realizableKE
v2f
)
```

The simulation is requesting `realizableKEpsilon` but the installed OpenFOAM version does not recognize this model name. Looking at the valid list, the correct name is **`realizableKE`** (without the `psilon`).

## Root Cause

In `constant/momentumTransport`, the user requirement specifies `realizableKEpsilon` but the actual keyword expected by this version of OpenFOAM is `realizableKE`. The term "kEpsilon" is part of the model name, but the valid list shows `realizableKE` (not `realizableKEpsilon`).

## Solution

**In `constant/momentumTransport`**, change the turbulence model entry from:

```
model           realizableKEpsilon;
```

to:

```
model           realizableKE;
```

This is the exact model name listed among the valid options. Everything else in the `momentumTransport` file (including `printCoeffs on;`) should remain unchanged.

Alternatively, if the user requirement truly insists on `kEpsilon`-based physics, another option would be to use `kOmegaSST` (also a two-equation model), but the simplest and most direct fix is simply using the correctly named `realizableKE` model that is already available in the valid list.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='constant/momentumTransport', changes='Change model from realizableKEpsilon to realizableKE')]
